name: Wayne-Westland
description_short: Wayne Westland Federal Credit Union is a Michigan-based credit union established in 1948, serving over 11,100 members with $148M in assets and a full range of banking services.
description_long:
Wayne Westland Federal Credit Union was established in 1948 and is headquartered in Westland, Michigan. The institution operates as a community-focused financial cooperative, serving a specific geographic membership base with essential banking services. Over the past 75+ years, it has grown to steward $148 million in assets and maintain a membership of 11,100+ loyal members supported by 34 dedicated employees.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking products including checking accounts (Share Draft with 7,000+ accounts), savings accounts (13,000+ Regular Savings accounts), money market accounts (400+ accounts), and certificates of deposit (600+ Share Certificates). Beyond deposit products, Wayne Westland provides credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and IRA/Keogh accounts. Digital banking services include online banking, mobile apps for iOS and Android, bill pay, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and online loan payments. The credit union also offers financial counseling, financial education workshops, and student scholarships.

What distinguishes Wayne Westland is its focus on community banking with a single brick-and-mortar branch location at 500 S Wayne Road in Westland, Michigan, supplemented by one ATM for member access. The institution emphasizes member education through financial workshops and financial tips. The credit union's membership eligibility is tied to geographic community participation, making it accessible to local residents and businesses seeking personalized service from a locally-based financial institution.

However, recent member reviews raise concerns about service reliability. A member reported experiencing a two-day outage with persistent "Error 500 Server Error" messages when attempting to access online banking, compounded by difficulty reaching the 24-hour phone support line. The institution's current operational status shows lobby and drive-thru hours as "currently closed," and there is at least one formal member complaint on record regarding system accessibility issues. With only one physical branch and limited ATM access compared to larger institutions, members may face convenience challenges. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mortgages (home purchase, refinance, and other options)
Online Banking and Mobile App (iOS and Android)
Bill Pay and Online Loan Payments
Financial Counseling and Financial Education Workshops
pros: Established in 1948 with 75+ years of operational history and stability
Serves over 11,100 members with $148 million in assets under management
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, auto loans, personal loans, credit cards, and mortgages
Mobile banking app available for both iOS and Android with online banking, bill pay, and remote deposit capture
Offers financial counseling, financial education workshops, and student scholarships
Community-focused institution serving specific geographic membership base with personalized service
Multiple account types available (13,000+ Regular Savings, 7,000+ Share Draft, 600+ CDs)
cons: Documented online banking outages with Error 500 Server Error affecting member access for extended periods
Difficulty reaching 24-hour phone support during reported service issues, limiting member assistance options
Single branch location with only one ATM limits physical accessibility compared to larger banking networks
Lobby and drive-thru hours currently showing as closed with unclear operational status for members
Limited geographic service area restricted to specific community membership eligibility
best_for: Westland, Michigan residents seeking locally-based community banking with personalized service
Members prioritizing financial education, counseling, and student scholarship programs
Consumers with auto loan or mortgage needs from a credit union with 75+ years of stability
Local businesses and residents eligible for the credit union's community-specific membership
diagnosis: Wayne Westland FCU is best suited for Westland-area residents and eligible community members seeking comprehensive banking services from an established, locally-rooted credit union with education-focused programs. However, potential members should carefully evaluate recent service reliability issues documented in member reviews before opening accounts, particularly if online banking access is essential to their banking needs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
